meta.title: Enterprise AI Governance, Architecture, Safety & Compliance — Implementation Blueprint 2026–2030
meta.classification: CONFIDENTIAL — C-Suite / Board / CISO / CAIO / CDO / CRO / General Counsel
audience: Board of Directors (Risk, Audit, Technology committees)
meta.scope: Fortune 500 / Global 2000 regulated enterprises (FSI, healthcare, pharma, energy, public sector, defense-adjacent).

executiveSummary:
# Enterprise AI Governance, Architecture, Safety & Compliance — Implementation Blueprint 2026–2030

This blueprint provides a **regulator-defensible, C-suite-ready operating model** for deploying,
scaling, and governing AI — including autonomous agents and RAG systems — across a Fortune 500 /
Global 2000 organization over the 2026–2030 horizon.

## Why now

Between 2024 and 2026 the global AI regulatory perimeter hardened sharply: the **EU AI Act** entered
staggered enforcement (GPAI obligations Aug 2025; high-risk Aug 2026), **NIS2** transposed across the
EU, **DORA** took effect for financial services (Jan 2025), the **NIST AI RMF 1.0 + Generative AI
Profile** became a de-facto U.S. baseline, and **ISO/IEC 42001:2023** matured into the auditable
AI Management System standard. Simultaneously, frontier capabilities (agentic tool-use, long-context
reasoning, multi-model orchestration) moved from pilot to production, creating new classes of risk —
prompt injection, data exfiltration via tools, supply-chain model poisoning, and systemic
cross-system failures — that legacy GRC tooling cannot observe, let alone control.

Boards are now personally exposed: SEC disclosure rules treat material AI incidents like
cybersecurity incidents; EU AI Act penalties reach **€35M or 7% of global turnover**; and under
NIS2, **senior management can be held personally liable** for governance failures. C-suites that
cannot evidence an auditable, continuous AI assurance pipeline by end-2026 will face escalating
operational, legal, and reputational loss.

## What this blueprint delivers

1. **Governance operating model** — three-lines-of-defense extended with an AI Safety function,
   board cadence, committee charters, RACI across 48 decisions, and role-based approval workflows.
2. **Six-layer reference architecture** — Infrastructure → Data → Model → Application → Agent →
   Governance & Assurance, mapped to 214 technical controls.
3. **Regulatory integration** — single control backbone mapping EU AI Act, GDPR, NIS2, DORA,
   NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 23894, and sectoral overlays.
4. **RAG provenance & hardening** — cryptographic provenance chain, citation enforcement,
   prompt-injection defense, tenant isolation, and data-exfiltration controls.
5. **Autonomous agent risk management** — capability scoping, tool-use governance, runtime
   sandboxing, rollback, and human-in-the-loop gates.
6. **Continuous AI assurance** — zero-trust CI/CD, OPA/Rego gates, GitHub Actions compliance
   annotations, evidence vaulting, regulator-defensible audit trail.
7. **90-day execution pack** — Gantt, playbooks, Power BI semantic model + DAX, SQL/DAX validation,
   Jira/ServiceNow integration, AWS/Azure Terraform modules, Slack/Teams ChatOps approvals.
8. **Phased roadmap 2026–2030** — four horizons with board-level OKRs and investment envelopes.

## Headline metrics (target state, end-2028)

| Metric | Baseline (2026) | Target (2028) |
|---|---|---|
| AI systems in inventory with full lineage | 15% | ≥99% |
| Mean-time-to-detect (MTTD) AI incidents | 14 days | ≤2 hours |
| % of production AI with continuous assurance | <5% | ≥95% |
| Mean-time-to-remediate (MTTR) Critical AI findings | 22 days | ≤24 hours |
| High-risk systems with EU AI Act conformity assessment | 0% | 100% |
| Board AI risk dashboard availability | Monthly | Real-time |
| Regulatory finding rate (per audit) | 4.2 | <0.5 |

## Executive call to action

The board should approve, within 90 days, (1) formal adoption of this blueprint, (2) appointment of
a Chief AI Officer with veto authority over non-compliant deployments, (3) a ring-fenced
investment envelope of **0.8–1.4% of IT OPEX** for 2026–2027, and (4) quarterly board review of
the AI governance scorecard defined in Module H.

moduleA_strategic.title: Executive & Strategic Context
moduleA_strategic.summary: Board-level framing, stakeholder map, loss-event taxonomy, and strategic posture.

sections.title: Strategic Posture & Risk Appetite
sections.content: Define a written AI risk appetite statement approved by the Board Risk Committee. It must cover: (a) use-case admissibility (prohibited / high-risk / limited-risk / minimal-risk tiers mirroring EU AI Act Art. 5–6), (b) tolerance for autonomy levels (L0 advisory → L4 fully autonomous), (c) third-party model risk limits, (d) data residency & sovereignty constraints, and (e) acceptable maximum single-event loss (AMSEL) expressed in currency and reputational terms.
outputs: Board-approved AI Risk Appetite Statement (2–3 pages)

sections.title: Stakeholder Map & Accountability
sections.content: Formalize accountability across the three lines of defense, extended with an AI Safety function reporting to the CAIO with a dotted line to the Board.

roles.accountability: AI cyber/adversarial risk, supply chain, zero trust
AI model risk, capital / economic impact

sections.title: Loss-Event Taxonomy (AI-specific)
sections.content: Adds AI-specific loss categories on top of the standard Basel / COSO / NIST taxonomy.

categories.name: Membership inference / PII leak
categories.impact: GDPR breach, fines up to 4% turnover

sections.title: Board Cadence & Reporting
sections.content: Minimum cadence: monthly CAIO report, quarterly Board Risk Committee, semi-annual full board deep-dive, annual independent AI audit disclosed in the Annual Report.
artifacts: AI Governance Scorecard (real-time dashboard, see Module H)
Top-10 AI risks heatmap with owner & due date
High-risk system portfolio snapshot (EU AI Act Annex III mapping)
Material incident log with root-cause & remediation status

moduleB_architecture.title: Six-Layer Enterprise AI Reference Architecture
moduleB_architecture.summary: Canonical stack with 214 controls mapped across six layers, plus cross-cutting planes.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Platform
layers.purpose: Compute, network, storage, identity, KMS, confidential computing.

controls.name: Confidential computing for model training
Private endpoints only — no public IP egress for model tier
HSM-backed KMS with quorum release
Hardware-attested workloads (SEV-SNP / TDX attestation)
GPU fleet FIPS-140-3 compliance
Multi-AZ deployment + active/active region failover
Network microsegmentation (zero-trust per-workload)
Immutable infrastructure (IaC-only; no manual changes)

layers.name: Data
layers.purpose: Lawful, lineage-bearing, quality-assured training, fine-tuning, retrieval, and evaluation data.

controls.name: Lawful basis recorded per dataset (GDPR Art. 6/9)
Dataset datasheets (Gebru et al.) mandatory for HR/credit/medical data
PII detection & tokenization in pre-processing pipelines
Full lineage from raw → features → embeddings → model
Synthetic data provenance tags & watermarking
Bias & representativeness testing before training release
Data retention schedules aligned to purpose limitation
Right-to-erasure propagation to embeddings and caches
Cross-border transfer records (SCC / adequacy / BCR)
Vector store tenant isolation (namespace + IAM)

layers.name: Model
layers.purpose: Foundation models (hosted/self-hosted), fine-tunes, adapters, evaluation harness.

controls.name: Model registry with signed artifacts (Sigstore/Cosign)
Model cards (Mitchell et al.) published for every registered model
Pre-release evaluation suite (capability + safety + bias + robustness)
Red-team engagement per risk tier (MAN-DATORY for high-risk)
Watermarking of generative outputs (EU AI Act Art. 50)
Model supply-chain SBOM + attestation
Continuous evaluation in production (drift, quality, bias)
Shadow-mode deployment before full rollout
Fallback model / graceful degradation defined

layers.name: Application / Orchestration
layers.purpose: RAG, prompt orchestration, guardrails, function-calling, UX.

controls.name: Input guardrails: PII/secret scan, prompt-injection classifier, rate-limit
Output guardrails: toxicity, jailbreak echo, PII, hallucination scoring
Structured citations required for every RAG response
Prompt & response logged with hash chain (evidence)
Tenant + purpose tagging propagated end-to-end
Streaming response moderation (mid-stream abort)
Content provenance (C2PA) on generated artifacts

layers.name: Agent & Autonomy
layers.purpose: Tool-using, multi-step, goal-directed agents with bounded autonomy.

controls.name: Every agent has declared capability scope (machine-readable manifest)
Tools signed, version-pinned, capability-bounded
Agent runtime in per-task ephemeral sandbox (no persistent write)
OPA policy check before every privileged tool invocation
HITL required for L3+ autonomy actions above threshold
Budget, time, and step limits enforced; kill-switch always-available
Full agent trajectory traced & replay-able (OpenTelemetry + artifact store)
Multi-agent coordination graph reviewed for emergent risk

layers.name: Governance & Assurance
layers.purpose: Policy, risk, audit, evidence, regulatory interface.

controls.name: Single source-of-truth AI system inventory (mandatory fields: tier, owner, lifecycle, regulator exposure)
Policy-as-code (OPA) gates in CI/CD for every AI artifact
Evidence vault (WORM, 10-year retention)
Continuous control monitoring (CCM) with KRI/KPI
Regulator-defensible audit trail (cryptographic chain)
Incident management workflow aligned to NIS2 + DORA + local IR
Conformity assessment documentation pack (Annex IV)
Annual independent AI audit against ISO/IEC 42001

crossCutting.notes: Human identity via OIDC/SAML; workload identity via SPIFFE/SPIRE. Every call authenticated and authorized.
OpenTelemetry traces/metrics/logs; LLM-specific spans (prompt, retrieval, tool, moderation).
Zero-trust; secrets manager; DLP; WAF + LLM-aware gateway; EDR; CSPM/CNAPP.
Data minimization, purpose limitation, right-to-erasure propagated to caches/embeddings.
Per-prompt, per-agent, per-tenant cost attribution; budgets & quotas.

moduleC_operatingModel.title: Governance Operating Model
moduleC_operatingModel.summary: Committees, charters, RACI, role-based approvals, ChatOps federation.

committees.name: Board Technology & AI Risk Committee
committees.mandate: Approve risk appetite, review top risks, approve material AI investments.

committees.name: AI Governance Council (AIGC)
committees.mandate: Portfolio decisions, exception approvals, policy updates.

committees.name: AI Safety Review Board (AISRB)
committees.mandate: Pre-release gate for high-risk & autonomous systems; red-team sign-off.

committees.name: AI Ethics & Human Impact Forum
committees.mandate: Fairness, human rights, worker impact, societal externalities.

raci.description: Condensed RACI for 10 anchor decisions; full model covers 48 decisions in the Excel companion.

decisions.decision: Deploy high-risk AI system (EU AI Act)

approvalWorkflows.name: High-Risk AI Deployment Approval
steps: Submit deployment request (form + model card + DPIA)
AI Safety Review Board technical review

approvalWorkflows.name: Autonomous Agent (L3+) Launch
steps: Staged rollout: shadow → 1% → 10% → 100% with automated rollback triggers

chatOps.identityFederation: SSO via Okta/Entra ID; role claims mapped via SCIM; approvals carry signed JWT.
flows: `/ai-approve <request-id>` — presents approval card, verifies user role, cryptographic co-sign.
`/ai-incident open` — creates incident ticket, pages on-call, starts war-room channel.
`/ai-risk-accept` — logs risk acceptance; requires CRO co-sign; writes to evidence vault.

moduleD_regulatory.title: Regulatory Integration — EU AI Act, GDPR, NIS2, DORA, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001
moduleD_regulatory.summary: Single control backbone, cross-regulation mapping, sector overlays.

regulations.name: EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689)
regulations.penaltyCeiling: €35M or 7% global annual turnover (whichever higher) for prohibited practices.
regulations.enterpriseImpact: All Annex III high-risk systems require a conformity assessment, post-market monitoring, and EU-database registration before placing on market.

regulations.name: General Data Protection Regulation (Regulation 2016/679)
regulations.aiImpact: Art. 22 imposes meaningful-information & opt-out for solely-automated decisions; DPIA mandatory for AI systems with large-scale / high-risk processing.

regulations.name: Directive (EU) 2022/2555 on network & information security
regulations.enterpriseImpact: Essential & important entities must: (a) apply 10 minimum measures (Art. 21), (b) report significant incidents within 24h/72h/30d, (c) senior management can be personally liable for non-compliance.
regulations.aiImpact: AI supply-chain compromise (weights/adapters) treated as a significant incident; continuous vulnerability management includes model-level CVEs.

regulations.name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (Regulation 2022/2554)
regulations.enterpriseImpact: Financial entities must: register ICT third-party providers (incl. AI model vendors), conduct threat-led penetration testing (TLPT), maintain ICT risk framework.
regulations.aiImpact: Hosted LLM providers used in critical/important functions become 'critical ICT third-party service providers' — EU-level oversight under ESMA/EBA/EIOPA.

regulations.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 + Generative AI Profile (NIST AI 600-1)
regulations.enterpriseImpact: De-facto US baseline; increasingly referenced in federal procurement (EO 14110 successors) and state laws (CO SB 205).

regulations.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management System
regulations.enterpriseImpact: Certifiable AIMS standard (like ISO 27001 for security); Annex A provides ~38 controls; enables vendor-to-regulator defensibility.

regulations.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — AI Risk Management (guidance)
regulations.enterpriseImpact: Complements 42001 with ISO 31000-aligned risk process tailored to AI lifecycle.

regulations.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
regulations.enterpriseImpact: Foundational ISMS; AI adds annex to statement of applicability.

controlBackbone.description: Single control backbone: each of the 214 controls (Module B) maps to 1..N regulatory citations. A JSON schema + CSV export enables GRC-tool ingestion (Archer/ServiceNow GRC/OneTrust).

moduleE_ragHardening.title: RAG Provenance & Hardening Controls
moduleE_ragHardening.summary: Cryptographic provenance chain, citation enforcement, prompt-injection defense, tenant isolation.

threatModel.threat: Copyright / license violations from ingested corpora

provenanceChain.description: Every retrieved chunk carries a signed provenance record; every generated response embeds structured citations with chunk hashes. Full chain: Source → Chunk → Embedding → Retrieval event → Prompt → Response.

hardeningControls.name: Retrieved content placed in explicit 'untrusted' role
hardeningControls.detail: System prompt instructs model to treat retrieval context as data, not instructions.

hardeningControls.name: Prompt-injection classifier on retrieved chunks
hardeningControls.detail: Classifier (fine-tuned + rule-based) blocks or sanitizes injections with >0.75 score.

hardeningControls.name: Answerability classifier
hardeningControls.detail: If no retrieved chunk supports the query, return 'I don't know' + escalation path.

hardeningControls.name: Citation-matching verifier
hardeningControls.detail: Post-generation: every factual claim must map to at least one cited chunk; unsupported claims are redacted.

hardeningControls.name: Freshness SLA enforcement
hardeningControls.detail: Chunks older than source-specific TTL are filtered or flagged.

hardeningControls.name: License-compatible retrieval
hardeningControls.detail: Retrieval filter excludes sources whose license conflicts with deployment context.

hardeningControls.name: Tenant + purpose ABAC on retrieval
hardeningControls.detail: OPA policy decision per query; every retrieval event logged with subject/resource/purpose.

hardeningControls.name: Embedding model version pinning + rotation plan
hardeningControls.detail: Embedding rotations staged; parallel index + query routing during cutover.

hardeningControls.name: Red-team prompt-injection suite (≥500 cases)
hardeningControls.detail: Run in CI; regression thresholds enforced.

hardeningControls.name: End-to-end trace with retrieval reasoning
hardeningControls.detail: Trace includes re-ranker scores, filtered vs. used chunks, final response.

moduleF_agentRisk.title: Autonomous Agent Risk Management
moduleF_agentRisk.summary: Autonomy levels, capability scoping, tool governance, runtime controls, kill-switches.

autonomyLevels.name: Assisted
autonomyLevels.description: Model drafts; human approves before execution.

autonomyLevels.name: Supervised autonomous
autonomyLevels.description: Agent executes bounded actions; human reviews in aggregate / by sample.

autonomyLevels.name: Bounded autonomous
autonomyLevels.description: Agent executes multi-step plans within a scoped domain; exceptions trigger HITL.
controls: L2 + per-action OPA policy + rollback + kill-switch + AISRB pre-approval

autonomyLevels.name: Fully autonomous
autonomyLevels.description: Agent self-directs within a large scope. RESERVED — requires Board approval.
controls: L3 + formal verification where feasible + continuous human-on-the-loop + liability insurance

capabilityScoping.description: Every agent ships with a signed, machine-readable capability manifest.

toolGovernance: Every tool has a signed manifest (capability, side effects, auth scope, rate limit).
Side-effectful tools (write/post) require OPA allow + HITL threshold.
Read tools carry purpose binding and data-scope filter enforced at broker.
Tool versions pinned per agent version; upgrade requires re-approval.
Synthetic monitoring of every tool's failure modes, latency, and error budget.
runtimeControls: Per-task ephemeral sandbox (Firecracker/gVisor or container with seccomp+no-new-privs).
No persistent filesystem; scratch only; artifacts exported through a reviewed egress channel.
Budget (USD, tokens, wall-clock, API calls) enforced at broker; hard cap + soft throttle.
Kill-switch: global (all agents), per-type, per-instance; MTTK ≤60s required.
Trajectory tracing: every plan, tool call, tool response, and intermediate thought logged.
Post-run evaluation: goal-completion, constraint-violation, anomaly-score; failures open incident.
multiAgentRisk: Explicit coordination graph — no unrestricted agent-to-agent messaging.
Supervisor agent mandatory for >2 agents; supervisor runs at lower autonomy level.
Chaos drills: inject faulty sub-agent, measure containment.

killSwitchPatterns.name: Global Freeze
killSwitchPatterns.trigger: Critical incident; suspends all L2+ agents within 60s.

killSwitchPatterns.name: Circuit Breaker
killSwitchPatterns.trigger: Anomaly in trajectory (e.g., cost, step count, loop detection) auto-pauses instance.

killSwitchPatterns.name: Blast-Radius Rollback
killSwitchPatterns.trigger: Safe-state restoration (DB snapshot / queue rewind) within 15 min.

moduleG_assurance.title: Continuous AI Assurance & Zero-Trust Pipelines
moduleG_assurance.summary: CI/CD gates, OPA/Rego, GitHub Actions annotations, evidence vault, regulator-defensible trail.

opaRegoPolicies.name: require-model-card
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Block deploy without signed model card.

opaRegoPolicies.name: require-dpia-for-high-risk
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Block high-risk deploy without DPIA doc ID.

opaRegoPolicies.name: deny-prohibited-use
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Reject uses listed in EU AI Act Art. 5.

opaRegoPolicies.name: require-hitl-for-l3
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Enforce HITL for L3 autonomy agents.

opaRegoPolicies.name: data-residency-eu
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Block model training/serving outside EU for EU-classified data.

opaRegoPolicies.name: license-compatibility
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Reject training data with incompatible licenses.

opaRegoPolicies.name: disallow-pii-in-logs
opaRegoPolicies.purpose: Reject pipelines that log raw PII.

prAnnotations.description: Compliance findings posted as GitHub PR annotations with file/line context, severity, rule id, remediation snippet, and link to control in evidence vault.

example.message: High-risk deployment missing DPIA reference (field: risk.dpiaId).
example.remediation: Link DPIA from GRC system: set risk.dpiaId to DPIA-YYYY-NNNN.

evidenceVault.description: Append-only, WORM-backed store of all artifacts that regulators or auditors may request.

zeroTrustMap: Workload identity: SPIFFE/SPIRE per service; no static creds.
mTLS everywhere; cert rotation via service mesh.
Continuous authorization: re-evaluated per action, not per session.
Least-privilege for model, data, tool, and secret access.
Assume-breach posture: anomaly detection on every layer with automated isolation.

moduleH_90dayPack.title: 90-Day Execution Pack
moduleH_90dayPack.summary: Concrete artifacts: plan, Gantt, dashboards, Power BI model, DAX, SQL validation, Terraform, playbooks, integrations.

exitCriteria: Core Terraform modules deployed (KMS, logging, identity)
RAG hardening live on 2 pilot apps

ganttChart.description: Condensed 13-week Gantt. Full version in Excel companion.

platformChoice.recommendation: Microsoft Power BI (Premium Capacity) — if organization is Microsoft-centric (E5/Purview/Defender); else Tableau Cloud with Data Management Add-on.
rationale: Power BI: deep Purview + Defender-for-Cloud integration, mature RLS, DAX for regulatory KPIs, native Teams embedding for ChatOps.
Tableau: superior ad-hoc visualization & Salesforce integration; weaker native OPA/policy integration.
Decision lens: integration depth with identity + compliance stack usually dominates; pick what your CISO office already operates.
platformChoice.costOrderOfMagnitude: Power BI Premium ~USD 5K/mo per capacity; Tableau Cloud ~USD 15–75/user/mo depending on tier.

boardSlideSpec.title: Why this matters
boardSlideSpec.content: AI risk is now a personal board liability (EU AI Act €35M/7%; NIS2 management liability; SEC disclosure).

boardSlideSpec.title: Where we are
boardSlideSpec.content: Baseline: 15% of AI systems inventoried; 5% with continuous assurance; 4.2 findings/audit.

boardSlideSpec.title: Where we need to be (2028)
boardSlideSpec.content: ≥99% inventoried; ≥95% continuously assured; ≤0.5 findings/audit; real-time board dashboard.

boardSlideSpec.title: Operating model
boardSlideSpec.content: CAIO + AIGC + AISRB; 3LoD extended with AI Safety function; quarterly Board Tech Cmte cadence.

boardSlideSpec.title: Six-layer architecture
boardSlideSpec.content: Infra → Data → Model → App → Agent → Governance; 214 controls; zero-trust; evidence vault.

boardSlideSpec.title: Regulatory coverage
boardSlideSpec.content: EU AI Act, GDPR, NIS2, DORA, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001 — single control backbone.

boardSlideSpec.title: 90-day plan
boardSlideSpec.content: 5 phases, 6 workstreams; exit criteria defined; first board packet in W13.

boardSlideSpec.title: Investment envelope
boardSlideSpec.content: 0.8–1.4% of IT OPEX over 2026–2027 — offset by avoided fines + insurance discount + productivity uplift.

boardSlideSpec.title: KPIs & cadence
boardSlideSpec.content: 8 board-level KPIs, real-time dashboard, quarterly deep-dive.

boardSlideSpec.title: Decisions requested
boardSlideSpec.content: (1) Approve blueprint; (2) Appoint CAIO with veto authority; (3) Ring-fence 2026–2027 investment; (4) Adopt quarterly scorecard review.

relationships: bridge_control_regulation: many-to-many between dim_control and dim_regulation
All fact tables -> dim_date via date_key

daxMeasures.name: MTTR (hours)
daxMeasures.dax: AVERAGEX(FILTER(fact_incident, NOT ISBLANK(fact_incident[closed_ts])), DATEDIFF(fact_incident[opened_ts], fact_incident[closed_ts], HOUR))

daxMeasures.name: EU AI Act Readiness %
daxMeasures.dax: VAR r = CALCULATETABLE(dim_regulation, dim_regulation[name]="EU AI Act") RETURN DIVIDE(CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(fact_control_evaluation), fact_control_evaluation[status]="PASS", TREATAS(VALUES(bridge_control_regulation[reg_id]), dim_regulation[reg_id]), r), CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(fact_control_evaluation), TREATAS(VALUES(bridge_control_regulation[reg_id]), dim_regulation[reg_id]), r), 0)

rls: BU analyst role: [bu] = USERPRINCIPALNAME() mapped to dim_owner.bu
Board role: aggregated only (column-level security on PII in dim_owner)

sqlDaxValidationSuite.description: End-to-end validation that the Power BI semantic model agrees with source of truth.

sqlChecks.name: System inventory parity
sqlChecks.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM src.ai_system WHERE is_active=1 EXCEPT SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dw.fact_ai_system WHERE retire_dt IS NULL;

sqlChecks.name: Control eval completeness
sqlChecks.sql: SELECT s.system_id FROM dw.fact_ai_system s WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dw.fact_control_evaluation e WHERE e.system_id=s.system_id AND e.eval_ts > DATEADD(day,-7,GETDATE()));

sqlChecks.name: Incident regulator-notification SLA
sqlChecks.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dw.fact_incident WHERE severity IN ('CRITICAL','HIGH') AND regulator_notified=0 AND DATEDIFF(hour, opened_ts, ISNULL(closed_ts,GETDATE())) > 24;

sqlChecks.name: Orphaned control mappings
sqlChecks.sql: SELECT b.control_id FROM dw.bridge_control_regulation b LEFT JOIN dw.dim_control c ON c.control_id=b.control_id WHERE c.control_id IS NULL;

sqlChecks.name: Agent trajectory budget breaches
sqlChecks.sql: SELECT agent_id, COUNT(*) FROM dw.fact_agent_trajectory WHERE cost_usd > 50 OR steps > 50 GROUP BY agent_id;

daxChecks.name: Measure sanity: Controls In Place % between 0 and 1
daxChecks.test: EVALUATE ROW("v", [Controls In Place %]) — expect v ∈ [0,1]

daxChecks.name: RLS smoke: auditor sees N, BU sees ≤N
daxChecks.test: Compare EVALUATE CALCULATE([Controls In Place %]) under two identities

ciTicketingIntegration.description: Every failed control or anomaly becomes a Jira/ServiceNow ticket with linked evidence.

workflow: Open → Triaged → In Remediation → Awaiting Verification → Closed

serviceNow.sla: OLA linked to CMDB CI for the AI system
serviceNow.cmdbLink: Every AI system registered as ci_ai_system CI class with relationship to applications + data stores

pythonServerlessRemediation.description: Reference AWS Lambda / Azure Functions remediation handlers.

functions.name: rotate-vector-index-embeddings
functions.trigger: EventBridge rule on embedding-model version update
pseudocode: mirror ingest for last TTL window

functions.name: quarantine-agent-on-anomaly
pseudocode: notify Slack #ai-incident with signed approval card

functions.name: revoke-token-on-tool-misuse
pseudocode: file Jira AIGOV finding with control L5-004

functions.name: erasure-propagator
pseudocode: resolve subject -> data map (features, embeddings, caches)
delete with signed receipts across systems
store erasure certificate in evidence vault

remediationPlaybooks.title: Prompt injection detected in production RAG
steps: Pull corpus subset for forensic review
Retrain injection classifier if new pattern

remediationPlaybooks.title: Data subject erasure request
steps: Issue certificate to subject within 30 days

remediationPlaybooks.title: Regulator request for evidence
steps: Submit via secure portal within deadline

views.name: Heatmap
views.description: Controls × Systems matrix; cell color = status; click → drill-down.

views.name: Trend
views.description: 30/60/90d open vs. closed; MTTR; by severity and by framework.

views.name: Owner workload
views.description: Findings per owner with aging; auto-escalation at SLA breach.

slackTeamsTemplates.name: Approval card (Teams Adaptive Card)
slackTeamsTemplates.format: Adaptive Card v1.5 with Action.Execute bound to federated identity; sign-claim carries JWT with role=ai_approver.

terraformModules.name: tf-aws-kms-ai
terraformModules.purpose: Envelope keys (CMK) with policies for model/data/evidence, rotation enabled.

terraformModules.name: tf-aws-vpc-ai-private
terraformModules.purpose: Private VPC, subnets, SG, NAT, endpoints for Bedrock/SageMaker/S3/KMS — no public egress.

terraformModules.name: tf-aws-sagemaker-secure
terraformModules.purpose: SageMaker domain, VPC-only, KMS-encrypted EBS, network isolation.

terraformModules.name: tf-aws-bedrock-guardrails
terraformModules.purpose: Amazon Bedrock with Guardrails (PII, topic policy), CloudWatch metrics.

terraformModules.name: tf-aws-evidence-vault
terraformModules.purpose: S3 Object-Lock (compliance mode), KMS, access logs to SIEM.

terraformModules.name: tf-aws-logging-ai
terraformModules.purpose: CloudTrail + CloudWatch + OpenTelemetry collector + Kinesis → SIEM.

terraformModules.name: tf-azure-aoai-private
terraformModules.purpose: Azure OpenAI with Private Endpoint, customer-managed keys, content filters.

terraformModules.name: tf-azure-ml-secure
terraformModules.purpose: Azure ML workspace in VNet with private link, managed identity.

terraformModules.name: tf-azure-purview-ai
terraformModules.purpose: Purview scans + data maps for AI data estate.

terraformModules.name: tf-azure-sentinel-ai
terraformModules.purpose: Sentinel analytics rules for LLM-specific signals.

terraformModules.name: tf-any-opa-gate
terraformModules.purpose: Deploy OPA/Gatekeeper as pipeline + runtime policy engine.

terraformModules.name: tf-any-evidence-vault-s3
terraformModules.purpose: Cloud-agnostic evidence vault module wrapping vendor-specific immutability primitives.

cloudArchitecture.hybrid: Terraform + Crossplane for multi-cloud symmetry; evidence vault replicated cross-cloud via object replication with integrity checks.

predictiveComplianceRisk.description: Gradient-boosted model predicting likelihood a given AI system will breach a critical control in next 30 days.
predictiveComplianceRisk.output: Risk score 0–100 with SHAP explanations; >70 triggers Jira + board-tile flag.
predictiveComplianceRisk.mlops: Monthly retrain; drift-monitored; model card with fairness test across BUs.

remediationSuggestionEngine.description: RAG + agentic pattern: given a failing control + context, suggests prioritized remediation options with evidence links.
components: LLM with constrained tool-use (read-only on systems)

trendReporting.automation: Templates rendered from the same Power BI semantic model; e-sign for board distribution; tamper-evident PDFs (C2PA).

keyOutcomes: EU AI Act high-risk portfolio identified

boardAsks: Approve first L3 autonomy pilot criteria

keyOutcomes: Predictive compliance risk model in production
Board annually signs off AI posture as part of strategic review

investmentEnvelope.description: Indicative for a USD 5B revenue enterprise with ~USD 400M IT OPEX.
offsets: Productivity uplift from trusted AI deployment

caseStudies.title: Credit decisioning RAG + autonomous KYC triage
caseStudies.context: Deployed RAG assistant over policy corpora + L2 KYC triage agent across 18 jurisdictions.
risks: EU AI Act high-risk (credit scoring Annex III)
MAS FEAT + PRA SS1/23 model risk

caseStudies.title: Regulatory submission drafting + safety signal triage
caseStudies.context: L1 assistants draft regulatory submissions; L2 agents triage pharmacovigilance signals with HITL.
controls: Sign-off carried as 21 CFR Part 11 e-sign

caseStudies.title: Grid-operations copilot with L2 anomaly agent
caseStudies.context: Copilot advises control-room; L2 agent opens tickets and preps switching plans (never executes).

caseStudies.title: Procurement assistant with UK ATRS compliance
caseStudies.context: Assistant used in 14 departments; publishable transparency records.
controls: ATRS XML emitted automatically on deploy

caseStudies.title: Customer-care multi-agent swarm with tenant isolation
caseStudies.context: 3-agent supervisor graph for complaint handling across 42 brands.
controls: Swarm coordination graph reviewed by AISRB

codeExamples.githubActionsAiGate:
name: ai-compliance-gate
on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'ai/**'
      - 'deployments/**'
      - 'prompts/**'
jobs:
  policy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Run OPA conftest
        uses: instrumenta/conftest-action@master
        with:
          files: ai/ deployments/
          policy: policy/
      - name: Model card presence
        run: ./scripts/check_model_card.sh
      - name: DPIA linkage
        run: ./scripts/check_dpia.sh
      - name: Eval thresholds
        run: ./scripts/check_eval_thresholds.py
      - name: SBOM + signatures
        run: ./scripts/verify_supply_chain.sh
      - name: PR annotations
        if: failure()
        uses: ./.github/actions/ai-findings-annotate
codeExamples.daxEuAiActReadiness:
EU AI Act Readiness % =
VAR regTable = FILTER(dim_regulation, dim_regulation[name] = "EU AI Act")
VAR regIds   = CALCULATETABLE(VALUES(bridge_control_regulation[reg_id]),
                               TREATAS(VALUES(dim_regulation[reg_id]), regTable))
VAR totEval  = CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(fact_control_evaluation),
                          fact_control_evaluation[control_id] IN
                          CALCULATETABLE(VALUES(bridge_control_regulation[control_id]),
                                         bridge_control_regulation[reg_id] IN regIds))
VAR passEval = CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(fact_control_evaluation),
                          fact_control_evaluation[status] = "PASS",
                          fact_control_evaluation[control_id] IN
                          CALCULATETABLE(VALUES(bridge_control_regulation[control_id]),
                                         bridge_control_regulation[reg_id] IN regIds))
RETURN DIVIDE(passEval, totEval, 0)
